quick_facts.label: Award and level
quick_facts.value: B.Sc. (Hons.) Horticulture is a four-year undergraduate horticulture degree under the ICAR Sixth Deans’ Committee curriculum framework.

quick_facts.label: Indicative entry subjects
quick_facts.value: The ICAR curriculum framework specifies 10+2 Science with PCM, PCB, Agriculture or Horticulture, subject to the applicable norms of the admitting State Agricultural University (SAU), ICAR or higher agricultural education institution.

quick_facts.label: ICAR-all-India admission route
quick_facts.value: For the 2025 cycle, B.Sc. (Hons.) Horticulture was included among programmes for CUET (ICAR-UG). The bulletin required 10+2 with at least three core subjects from Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology and Agriculture/Inter-Agriculture; university-specific conditions could still apply.

quick_facts.label: Curriculum scale
quick_facts.value: The Sixth Deans’ Committee model allocates 167 graded credits plus 10 MOOCs credits, for a stated total of 177 credit hours; universities may modify an individual course by up to 30% with competent approval.

quick_facts.label: Practical learning
quick_facts.value: The model curriculum includes practical and skill courses, a study tour, and a 20-credit final-semester Student READY component that can include RAWE, industrial attachment, experiential learning, hands-on training, project work and internship.

quick_facts.label: Multiple-exit design
quick_facts.value: Under the proposed ICAR framework, students who complete the relevant requirements and a 10-week internship may exit after year 1 with a UG Certificate in Horticulture or after year 2 with a UG Diploma in Horticulture; the B.Sc. (Hons.) award follows completion of the four-year requirements.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: The national ICAR curriculum model describes first-year entry as 10+2 Science with PCM, PCB, Agriculture or Horticulture, while expressly leaving room for the rules of the relevant SAU, ICAR or higher agricultural education institution. Applicants should therefore treat a particular university’s prospectus as controlling.

For CUET (ICAR-UG) 2025, the official eligibility bulletin listed B.Sc. (Hons.) Horticulture and required candidates to have passed Class 12 with at least three core subjects chosen from Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology and Agriculture/Inter-Agriculture. It also prescribed 50% aggregate marks for General, OBC (NCL), UPS and EWS candidates and 40% for SC, ST, Third Gender and PwBD candidates. These are dated 2025-cycle requirements, not a substitute for the admission-year bulletin.
bullets: Confirm the university’s subject combination, domicile, category, age and any programme-specific requirements before applying.

Candidates using the 2025 ICAR counselling route had to take CUET subjects aligned with the subjects passed at Class 12; different examination subjects could result in rejection at counselling/admission.

sections.heading: Duration and qualification structure
paragraphs: The standard ICAR Sixth Deans’ Committee design is a four-year B.Sc. (Hons.) Horticulture programme. It specifies award of the degree after successful completion of the four-year requirements.

The framework also proposes NEP-aligned exit awards: a UG Certificate in Horticulture after the first year plus a 10-week internship, and a UG Diploma in Horticulture after the second year plus a 10-week internship. Whether and how these exits are operationalised should be checked with the admitting university.
bullets: Year 1 exit: UG Certificate in Horticulture, with the prescribed internship.
Year 2 exit: UG Diploma in Horticulture, with the prescribed internship.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: The ICAR model spans horticultural production and management across fruit, vegetables, flowers, spices, plantation crops and nursery systems, alongside agricultural sciences, business and technology. Early courses include Fundamentals of Horticulture; plant propagation and nursery management; commercial flower, spice and plantation-crop production; irrigation; and entrepreneurship and business management.

Later coursework covers commercial fruit and vegetable production, protected cultivation and precision farming, horticultural crop disease and pest management, urban and peri-urban horticulture, farm machinery, agricultural informatics and artificial intelligence, breeding, soil fertility, climate change, processing and value addition. In the seventh semester, the model provides 20-credit elective pathways in Fruit Science, Vegetable Science, or Floriculture and Landscaping.

This is a national model curriculum rather than an identical syllabus guarantee: the Committee permits universities and higher agricultural education institutions to adapt individual courses, within stated limits and approvals.
bullets: Skill-enhancement courses are concentrated in the first two years.
A 10–14-day study tour is scheduled in semester 5 in the model.
Semester 8 is dedicated to Student READY practical exposure, such as RAWE, industrial attachment, experiential learning or internship.

sections.heading: Admission routes
paragraphs: Admission is institution-specific. For the ICAR all-India counselling route in 2025, B.Sc. (Hons.) Horticulture was offered through CUET (ICAR-UG), with online application through the NTA CUET-UG process and seat allotment through online counselling based on merit rank and fulfilment of eligibility requirements.

Not every horticulture university necessarily follows the same route. For example, Dr Y.S. Parmar University of Horticulture and Forestry described entrance-test-based admission for normal undergraduate seats and Class 12 marks in English, Physics, Chemistry and Biology/Mathematics for self-financing seats. This illustrates why applicants must read the current prospectus for each target institution rather than rely solely on the ICAR route.
bullets: For an ICAR counselling route, check the current NTA/ICAR information bulletin, subject selection rules, participating universities and counselling schedule.
For direct or state-university routes, check that university’s current prospectus for its entrance examination, merit criteria, reservations and fee payment process.
Do not rely on the 2025 dates, cut-offs or application windows; admissions calendars and participating institutions can change each cycle.

sections.heading: Further study, examinations and career directions
paragraphs: A common academic progression is postgraduate study through ICAR AIEEA (PG). NTA’s 2025 notice states that ICAR AIEEA (PG) is conducted for admission to postgraduate degree programmes; the relevant year’s bulletin determines discipline mapping, qualifying-degree requirements and other eligibility conditions.

The degree’s applied curriculum supports horticulture-enterprise and farm-management pathways, including nursery, plantation, protected-cultivation, production, post-harvest and value-addition work. The National Career Service describes horticulture management work as involving crop and soil/climate selection, resource procurement, cultivation supervision, harvesting and post-harvest management, business planning and worker supervision; it identifies farm owners and agricultural cooperatives as probable employers. The listed senior management occupation is experience-dependent, so it should not be read as an entry-level salary or placement guarantee.

Recruitment examinations must be checked notification by notification. IBPS’s current CRP-SPL XVI notification includes Agricultural Field Officer (Scale I), and NABARD’s 2025 Grade A material included a specialist Plantation and Horticulture mains syllabus. A B.Sc. (Hons.) Horticulture holder should verify the exact degree wording, age, nationality, marks and other conditions in the active notification before applying. State Horticulture Officer recruitment is likewise conducted under individual state notifications rather than one uniform national examination.
bullets: Postgraduate route: ICAR AIEEA (PG), subject to that cycle’s eligibility and counselling rules.
Banking/rural-finance route: IBPS Agricultural Field Officer or NABARD Grade A only where the active notification accepts the candidate’s qualification.
Enterprise route: nursery, protected cultivation, plantation/farm operations, value addition and agribusiness; practical experience and local licences/compliance may be required.
